title: Wet-felted glasses case
subtitle: A firm rectangular merino case to fit standard spectacle frames, wet-felted over a card resist
excerpt: A rectangular merino case, approximately 17 × 8 cm, wet-felted over a stiff card resist and finished with a simple fold-over flap. The result is a padded, scratch-resistant case that fits most standard spectacle frames.
sourceNotes: Wet-felted spectacle cases are a standard introductory resist project in UK felting courses. Method synthesised from general wet-felting and flat-resist technique.

glossaryTerms.definition: A stiff form placed inside roving layers to prevent fibres bonding in one area, leaving a hollow or opening in the finished piece.
Combed and drawn wool fibres in a continuous rope, ready for wet felting.
The final stage of wet felting in which vigorous agitation compresses the fabric to its intended density and size.
Wool that has begun to bond but is not yet fully dense. The surface holds together but the piece can still be manipulated and shaped.

content.text: A wet-felted glasses case is made over a card
resist
: a piece of stiff card cut to the dimensions of the glasses, which prevents the
roving
from bonding in the opening of the case. The case is formed in two flat panels laid over each side of the resist, felted together around the edges, then opened and
fulled
to a firm, protective finish. The card resist is removed before drying and the glasses are then dried inside the case to set the shape. A fold-over flap at the top keeps the glasses in place.

items.name: Merino roving, contrasting colour (optional border or detail)
Stiff card (cereal box or similar), cut to 19 × 9 cm

content.text: Method
Step 1: Cut the resist and prepare layers

Cut the card resist to 19 × 9 cm (this will shrink to approximately 17 × 8 cm in the finished case). Cover both sides of the card resist with a thin layer of cling film or a plastic bag so the roving does not bond to the card during felting.
Step 2: Lay roving on the first side

Place the covered resist flat on the felting mat. Pull thin wisps of roving and lay them over the resist in a horizontal direction, extending the roving at least 2 cm beyond all four edges. Add a vertical layer on top. Add a third horizontal and a fourth vertical layer. The layers should cover the resist evenly without thin spots. Fold the overhanging roving over the edge of the resist and press it flat against the resist's underside.
Step 3: Add roving to the second side

Flip the resist over so the side with the folded roving faces down. Lay four fresh layers of roving on the new top side in the same way (horizontal, vertical, horizontal, vertical). Fold the overhanging roving over the remaining edges of the resist. Ensure both sides of the resist are covered and the edges are wrapped completely around from both sides.
Step 4: Wet and rub to pre-felt

Mix soapy water and drizzle it over both sides of the roving-covered resist until completely saturated. Place the felting net over the top surface and rub in small circular movements with flat hands, starting gently and increasing pressure after 3 minutes. Rub for 8 minutes on the first side. Flip the piece, place the net over the other side, and rub for a further 8 minutes. The piece has reached
pre-felt
stage when the surface does not pull apart when pinched.
Step 5: Remove the resist and full

Carefully slide the card resist out through one of the short ends. At this stage the felt is stable enough to hold its shape but still flexible. Roll the case in the bamboo mat and roll back and forth for 5 minutes. Unroll, turn 90 degrees, roll again. Repeat until the case is firm and the opening measures approximately 17 × 8 cm. Throw the case against the felting mat ten times from a short height to complete the
fulling
Step 6: Trim and dry over the glasses

Trim the open top edge to a straight line. Rinse the case in cool water, squeeze out excess, and slip a pair of glasses inside to set the shape while drying. The case will dry around the exact profile of the glasses. Leave flat on a towel for 24 hours or until fully dry before removing the glasses.

items.problem: The two sides of the case have bonded together around the resist
items.cause: Roving extended too far over the resist edges and the layers from both sides joined
items.fix: On future cases, keep the edge fold to 1.5 cm maximum. If the sides have joined along the short edge, slip scissors inside and cut the bond carefully. The thick felt will not fray.

items.problem: The opening is too small to slide glasses in easily
items.cause: Fulling was done after the resist was removed for too long, closing the opening
items.fix: Before fully drying, stretch the opening gently by hand and insert a book of the correct thickness to hold it open while drying. The felt will hold the stretched shape.

items.problem: The surface has thin patches where the glasses will press
items.cause: Roving layers were uneven during laying
items.fix: Add small patches of roving to the thin areas while still damp and needle-felt them into the surface with a size 38 felting needle to blend the edges.

content.text: Care

Spot-clean with a damp cloth. If the case needs washing, hand-wash in cool water with a small amount of wool-safe detergent, reshape while damp with the glasses inside, and dry flat. Do not machine wash.
